name: principles
description: Provides development principles, guidelines, and VibeCoder guidance. Use when user mentions 原則, principles, ガイドライン, guidelines, VibeCoder, 安全性, safety, 差分編集, diff-aware. Triggers: 原則, principles, ガイドライン, VibeCoder, 安全性, 差分編集. Do not use for actual implementation - use impl skill instead.
capabilities: Provide development principles and safety guidelines for all coding tasks
Guide minimal diff-aware file editing to preserve existing code structure
Help read and understand repository context including README and Plans.md
Support VibeCoders (non-technical users) with natural language guidance
Enforce safety rules like dry-run mode and protected path handling
Route requests to appropriate sub-skills based on user intent
author: Chachamaru127
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only meta-skill containing markdown files with development principles and guidelines. All static findings are false positives because bash commands and file operations shown in documentation are educational examples, not executable code. The skill provides safe, read-only guidance for AI-assisted development workflows.
valueStatement: AI coding assistants need clear principles to work safely and effectively. This skill provides development guidelines, safety rules, and VibeCoder support for non-technical users to guide AI-assisted development.
userTitle: Apply Development Principles and Safety Guidelines
limitations: This skill only provides guidance, it does not execute code changes itself
Safety mode enforcement depends on external configuration files
Does not provide implementation code, only principles and guidelines

useCases.title: Establish Safe Workflows
useCases.description: Apply consistent safety principles and minimal diff editing to prevent code breakage

useCases.title: Guide VibeCoders
useCases.description: Help non-technical users understand AI actions and what to say next

useCases.title: Standardize Practices
useCases.description: Ensure all AI-assisted work follows consistent development principles and safety rules

promptTemplates.title: Apply Safety Principles
promptTemplates.prompt: What safety principles should I follow before editing this file?

promptTemplates.title: Check Repository Context
promptTemplates.prompt: Read the repository context and tell me about the project structure and current state

promptTemplates.title: Edit Minimally
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the best practices for making minimal diff edits to preserve existing code?

promptTemplates.title: Help VibeCoder
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm not technical. What should I say to get the AI to work on my project?

bestPractices: Read file content before editing to understand the existing code structure
Apply minimal diff principles to keep changes focused and reviewable
Check git status and git diff after every change to verify modifications
antiPatterns: Using Write tool to overwrite entire files instead of using Edit for targeted changes
Mixing unrelated changes (formatting, refactoring, feature additions) in one edit
Ignoring project configuration like safety settings and protected paths

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It is a meta-skill containing sub-skills for different purposes.

faq.question: Does this skill make changes to my codebase?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides guidance and principles. It does not execute code changes or access files beyond reading documentation.

faq.question: How does VibeCoder mode help non-technical users?
faq.answer: VibeCoder mode translates technical questions into simple language and provides examples of what users should say to guide the AI.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads markdown documentation files. It does not access .env files, credentials, or any user data.

faq.question: Why do I see bash command examples in the documentation?
faq.answer: These are example commands shown to the AI assistant for reference. The AI reads them to understand context, but you do not run them directly.

faq.question: How does this differ from other development skills?
faq.answer: This skill provides foundational principles and safety guidelines. Other implementation skills actually write code. This skill ensures safe, consistent workflows.
